mnemonics: S.A.M.P.L.E. (Simple Attributes: Measuring Population Likelihood Estimates)
A-B-C Sampling: Always Be Counting attributes in a sample to infer about the population
keyPoints: Simple sampling of attributes involves drawing conclusions about a population proportion based on a sample proportion.
The success-failure condition (np >= 10 and n(1-p) >= 10) must be checked to ensure the sampling distribution is approximately normal.
The standard error for the sample proportion is calculated as sqrt(p(1-p)/n).
commonMistakes: Confusing the sample proportion (p-hat) with the population proportion (p).
Failing to verify the success-failure condition before using normal approximation.
Using the sample proportion p-hat instead of the hypothesized p when calculating the standard error for hypothesis tests.
